name: Paris Fashion Week (Ready-to-Wear)
description: The pinnacle of the Big Four fashion weeks, showcasing the world's top ready-to-wear collections at venues across Paris including the Palais de Tokyo and Tuileries Garden.

name: Paris Haute Couture Week
description: The exclusive showcase of haute couture collections by members of the Chambre Syndicale, representing the highest echelon of fashion craftsmanship.

name: Milan Fashion Week
description: Italy's premier fashion event organized by Camera Nazionale della Moda Italiana, celebrated for luxury craftsmanship and iconic houses like Gucci, Prada, and Versace.

name: New York Fashion Week
description: The first stop on the Big Four circuit, NYFW is the most commercially driven fashion week, setting global trends in ready-to-wear and streetwear.

name: London Fashion Week
description: Organized by the British Fashion Council, LFW is renowned for nurturing avant-garde and emerging designers alongside established houses like Burberry and Vivienne Westwood.

name: Tokyo Fashion Week (Rakuten FWT)
description: Japan's leading fashion showcase blending traditional aesthetics with futuristic streetwear innovation, featuring brands like Comme des Garcons and Issey Miyake.

name: Shanghai Fashion Week
description: China's largest fashion event at Xintiandi, driving the rise of Chinese luxury and contemporary design with a blend of Eastern heritage and Western influences.

name: Seoul Fashion Week
description: South Korea's flagship fashion event at Dongdaemun Design Plaza, fueling the global K-fashion wave with innovative streetwear and digital-first presentations.

name: Sao Paulo Fashion Week
description: Latin America's most influential fashion week and the fifth-largest globally, known for vibrant color, sustainability focus, and Brazilian beachwear innovation.

name: Berlin Fashion Week
description: Germany's cutting-edge fashion platform emphasizing sustainability, tech fashion, and underground street culture from Europe's creative capital.

name: Sydney Fashion Week (Afterpay AFW)
description: Australia's premier resort-wear showcase, pioneering the 'see now buy now' model with a focus on relaxed luxury and sustainability.

name: Lagos Fashion Week
description: West Africa's leading fashion platform championing African design talent, traditional textiles, and the continent's booming creative economy.

name: Lakme Fashion Week
description: India's largest fashion event showcasing both traditional Indian craftsmanship and contemporary design, a gateway for emerging South Asian designers.

name: Copenhagen Fashion Week
description: Scandinavia's leading fashion event, globally recognized for setting sustainability standards with its mandatory requirements for participating brands.

name: Tbilisi Fashion Week (MBFW Tbilisi)
description: Georgia's rising fashion scene at the crossroads of Europe and Asia, gaining international attention for bold, conceptual design that blends post-Soviet identity with ancient craft.

name: Dakar Fashion Week
description: One of Africa's oldest fashion events celebrating Francophone West African design, rich textile traditions, and pan-African creativity.

name: Arab Fashion Week
description: The Middle East's premier couture and ready-to-wear showcase, bridging Eastern opulence with global luxury fashion from the region's style capital.

name: Moscow Fashion Week (MBFW Russia)
description: Russia's primary fashion event spotlighting Eastern European design talent and luxury market trends from the CIS region.

name: Amsterdam Fashion Week
description: The Netherlands' biannual fashion showcase emphasizing Dutch design innovation, denim heritage, and sustainable fashion practices.

name: Madrid Fashion Week (MBFW Madrid)
description: Spain's leading fashion week at IFEMA showcasing Iberian designers and the Mediterranean aesthetic, with a growing focus on sustainability.

name: Johannesburg Fashion Week (SA Fashion Week)
description: Southern Africa's premier fashion platform promoting the continent's design diversity, from Shweshwe prints to contemporary African streetwear.

name: Bangkok International Fashion Week
description: Thailand's showcase for Southeast Asian design blending Thai silk heritage, tropical aesthetics, and emerging streetwear culture.

name: Jakarta Fashion Week
description: Indonesia's largest fashion event featuring batik innovation, modest fashion, and the world's biggest Muslim fashion market.

name: Premiere Vision Paris
description: The world's leading textile trade show where 1,800+ exhibitors preview fabrics, trims, and trends 18 months ahead of retail. The industry's trend-forecasting bible.

name: Texworld Paris
description: International textile sourcing fair in Paris focusing on fabrics from Asia, Turkey, and emerging markets, running alongside Premiere Vision.

name: Texworld USA
description: North America's largest textile sourcing event at the Javits Center, connecting global fabric suppliers with American brands and designers.

name: MAGIC Las Vegas
description: America's largest fashion trade show encompassing apparel, footwear, and accessories across the Las Vegas Convention Center, setting the commercial fashion agenda.

name: Canton Fair (Textiles & Garments)
description: The world's largest trade fair by attendance, with a massive textiles and garments section connecting Chinese manufacturers with global buyers.

name: Intertextile Shanghai Apparel Fabrics
description: Asia's largest apparel fabrics and accessories sourcing event, showcasing innovations from 4,500+ exhibitors at the National Exhibition Center.

name: Pitti Immagine Uomo
description: The world's most prestigious menswear trade show at Fortezza da Basso, a barometer of men's fashion trends and Italian craftsmanship.

name: Pitti Filati
description: The leading international yarn fair, showcasing fiber and knitwear innovations for the global fashion industry from Florence.

name: ISPO Munich
description: The world's leading trade fair for sporting goods and sportswear, driving technical fabric innovation and athleisure trends.

name: India International Garment Fair
description: India's largest garment sourcing fair showcasing the subcontinent's vast textile manufacturing capabilities and handloom heritage.

name: Vicenzaoro
description: Europe's premier jewelry trade fair in Vicenza, the heart of Italian goldsmithing, showcasing fine jewelry, watches, and precious gemstones.

name: JCK Las Vegas
description: North America's largest jewelry industry event at The Venetian, connecting 2,300+ exhibitors with retailers, designers, and buyers from 100+ countries.

name: Hong Kong Jewellery & Gem Fair
description: The world's largest fine jewelry event by exhibitor count, serving as Asia's premier trading hub for gemstones, pearls, and finished jewelry.

name: Baselworld / Watches and Wonders Geneva
description: The most prestigious luxury watch and jewelry fair in the world, where Swiss and international maisons unveil their latest horological masterpieces.

name: Istanbul Jewelry Show
description: Turkey's leading jewelry fair connecting Middle Eastern, Central Asian, and European markets at the crossroads of continents.

name: Jaipur Jewellery Show
description: India's premier gemstone and jewelry exhibition from the 'Pink City,' showcasing Rajasthani craftsmanship, Kundan, Meenakari, and precious colored stones.

name: Bangkok Gems & Jewelry Fair
description: Southeast Asia's largest gemstone and jewelry trade event, Thailand being one of the world's top colored gemstone trading hubs.

name: Mexico Fashion Week (Mercedes-Benz FW Mexico)
description: Mexico's leading fashion platform celebrating Latin American design, indigenous textile traditions, and the growing Mexican luxury market.

name: Stockholm Fashion Week
description: Sweden's minimalist fashion showcase known for clean Scandinavian design, sustainable practices, and brands like Acne Studios and H&M Studio.

name: Kuala Lumpur Fashion Week
description: Malaysia's premier fashion showcase highlighting modest fashion innovation, batik heritage, and Southeast Asian contemporary design.

name: Colombiamoda
description: Latin America's second-largest fashion trade show in Medellin, showcasing Colombian textile innovation, swimwear, and intimate apparel manufacturing.

name: Hyeres International Festival of Fashion
description: France's most prestigious emerging designer competition at Villa Noailles, launching careers of avant-garde talent in fashion, photography, and accessories.

name: Taipei Fashion Week
description: Taiwan's emerging fashion platform showcasing East Asian contemporary design alongside traditional Hakka and indigenous textile craftsmanship.

name: Colombo Fashion Week
description: Sri Lanka's premier fashion event showcasing South Asian design talent, handloom textiles, and the island's apparel manufacturing expertise.
